expectations.text: Pipeline generation: primary KPI is 400 net-new MQLs with an implied CPL target at or below $187.50 ($75K/400) -- the plan benchmarks against the historical $180 CPL from the benchmark report campaign and explains how channel allocation will hit or beat that efficiency
expectations.evidence: Primary KPI: '400 net-new MQLs' with CPL target '<= $187.50 (benchmark: $180 from previous campaign)'. The goal header states '$75,000 | 400 net-new MQLs at <= $187.50 CPL'. Channel allocations include per-channel CPL estimates (syndication at $100-$125 CPL) and explicitly reference beating the $180 benchmark.

expectations.text: Budget efficiency: channel mix allocates $75,000 with per-channel line items, excludes paid search and events as instructed, and weights spend toward the research-driven content format that historically produced 280 MQLs -- not toward blog content which was explicitly called out as ineffective
expectations.evidence: Budget sums to $75,000 across 8 line items. No paid search or events included. Research report production receives $12,000 + $10,000 derivative content. The brief states 'Blog content alone has never moved the needle' and 'Explicitly avoids standalone blog content... by ensuring every blog post is a derivative of the research report with a CTA back to the gated asset.'

expectations.text: Content leverage: lead magnet is anchored in original research (benchmark report, state-of-industry survey, or similar data-driven asset) that builds on the proven benchmark report format, with a clear plan to derive webinar topics, LinkedIn content, email nurture sequences, and syndication assets from that single research pillar
expectations.evidence: Lead magnet: '2026 State of AI in Revenue Operations Benchmark Report' based on survey of 200+ RevOps leaders. Derivative content plan explicitly described: 'one research investment, many distribution formats' producing webinar content (3 sessions each tied to a report finding), LinkedIn posts (2/week), email nurture (8-touch sequence), syndication, infographics, and blog summaries.

expectations.text: Alignment: sales-marketing integration defines a specific MQL scoring threshold that triggers Salesforce routing to the six mid-market AEs, specifies the Outreach sequence for MQL follow-up, and sets a measurable speed-to-lead SLA -- connecting marketing's 400-MQL target to AE pipeline activity
expectations.evidence: MQL threshold: 30 points AND firmographic fit. Salesforce routing via round-robin to six AEs. Outreach 'Thought Leadership MQL' 4-touch 10-day sequence specified. Speed-to-lead SLA: HOT MQL (score >= 60) within 4 hours, Standard MQL within 24 hours. Weekly MQL-to-SAL conversion review at 40% target.

expectations.text: Execution velocity: content calendar fits within the capacity of two content marketers and a half-time designer without overcommitting -- production milestones are sequenced so the research asset is complete before derivative content begins, and no week exceeds feasible output
expectations.evidence: Content calendar sequences research report production (Weeks 1-4, CM1 leads) before derivative content (Weeks 5-12). Capacity check confirms CM1 heaviest in Weeks 1-4, CM2 at steady 2-3 pieces/week. Designer half-time (~20 hrs/week) with heaviest weeks at 10 hrs for ad creative and report layout. Explicit feasibility assessment per role.

expectations.text: Budget efficiency: risk and contingency section includes a time-bound decision gate with a specific MQL pace threshold (e.g., below 100 MQLs by Week 4), a named metric to evaluate, and a prescribed budget reallocation from underperforming to outperforming channels -- preventing quarter-end scramble on a $75K spend
expectations.evidence: Decision gate: 'If fewer than 100 MQLs generated by Week 4' with five prescribed actions: (1) evaluate channel-level CPL, (2) pause channels with CPL > $250, (3) reallocate to channels with CPL < $150, (4) increase syndication by up to $8,000, (5) accelerate Webinar 2 from Week 9 to Week 7.
